entry.title: Overcoming Challenges in Machine Learning with SAP Solutions
entry.content:
Machine learning (ML) is no longer just a futuristic buzzword—it’s here, transforming industries and reshaping enterprise software solutions. When paired with SAP systems, particularly SAP S/4HANA, machine learning has the potential to unlock powerful insights, streamline processes, and revolutionize how businesses operate. However, integrating machine learning with SAP solutions comes with its fair share of challenges.

Understanding Machine Learning Integration in SAP Solutions

The Role of Machine Learning in SAP S/4HANA

SAP S/4HANA, a leading ERP solution, has embedded machine learning capabilities that allow businesses to:

  • Analyze vast datasets in real time.
  • Make predictive decisions across financials, supply chain, and customer relationship management.
  • Automate repetitive tasks, freeing up time for strategic activities.

Drawing from our experience, the integration of ML within SAP S/4HANA is a game-changer for organizations seeking a data-driven competitive edge.

Overview of Embedded Machine Learning Capabilities

SAP systems come equipped with tools like SAP AI Core and SAP AI Business Services, which include pre-built machine learning models for:

  • Invoice matching in finance.
  • Demand forecasting in supply chain.
  • Sentiment analysis in customer service.

After trying out these tools, our findings show that businesses can accelerate their ML adoption without building algorithms from scratch.

Benefits of Integrating Machine Learning with ERP Systems

When machine learning meets ERP systems like SAP, the results are impactful:

  • Improved decision-making: Predictive analytics help businesses anticipate market trends.
  • Cost efficiency: Automation reduces manual workloads and minimizes errors.
  • Enhanced customer satisfaction: Personalized recommendations and faster issue resolutions improve the customer experience.

For instance, companies like Unilever leverage SAP’s ML features to optimize inventory and reduce waste in their supply chain.

Challenges in Implementing Machine Learning

While machine learning offers immense potential, implementation in SAP environments is not without challenges.

Identifying Common Obstacles Faced During Integration

  1. Data Quality Issues:
    Poor data quality or fragmented datasets can lead to inaccurate predictions.
    • Real-life example: A retail company using SAP faced inaccurate forecasting because historical sales data was incomplete.
  2. Compliance and Security Risks:
    Handling sensitive data (e.g., financial or customer data) requires robust security measures to meet regulations like GDPR.
  3. Skill Gaps in Teams:
    Many teams lack the expertise to effectively implement and maintain ML models in SAP systems.
  4. Integration Complexity:
    Existing systems often require significant customization to work with ML algorithms.

Strategies to Overcome Data Quality and Compliance Issues

  • Data Quality:
    Invest in data cleansing tools like SAP Data Intelligence to integrate, validate, and enrich your datasets.
    • Our team discovered through using SAP Data Intelligence that it significantly improved ML model accuracy.
  • Compliance:
    Use built-in security features in SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) to protect sensitive information and ensure regulatory compliance.
  • Skill Building:
    Upskill your team with SAP’s online training resources or partner with experienced consultants.

Key Machine Learning Use Cases in SAP

1. Predictive Analytics for Business Forecasting

Machine learning enables predictive modeling for accurate demand forecasting, helping businesses plan inventory, staffing, and production more effectively.

  • Example: After conducting experiments with SAP Predictive Analytics, a manufacturing company reduced overstock costs by 15%.

2. Enhancing Supply Chain Visibility Through AI and ML

Machine learning improves supply chain operations by providing real-time insights into potential disruptions.

  • Case Study: Retail giant Walmart uses SAP ML features to track shipments and optimize delivery routes.

3. Improving Telehealth Operations with Machine Learning

The healthcare industry, particularly telehealth services, is experiencing a revolution through SAP’s machine learning capabilities. Predictive analytics can optimize patient care by forecasting appointment no-shows and enabling better resource allocation. Moreover, natural language processing (NLP) models embedded in SAP Conversational AI can enhance virtual consultations by enabling real-time translation and symptom analysis.

According to Abto Software, telehealth consultations increased by 154% during the COVID-19 pandemic, highlighting the importance of scalable AI-driven tools to manage the surge in patient demand. Additionally, AI-powered platforms like the Zoom extension for telehealth have shown how seamless integration can improve both accessibility and efficiency for healthcare providers.

Our findings show that telehealth providers using SAP solutions have improved patient satisfaction and reduced operational inefficiencies, particularly in areas like appointment scheduling and patient triage. For example, a telehealth company partnered with SAP to integrate ML-driven predictive models, which resulted in a 20% reduction in missed appointments and a more efficient allocation of medical staff.

By integrating these healthcare advancements, SAP demonstrates how machine learning isn’t just about logistics or finance—it’s also transforming lives in critical industries like health.

Machine Learning Algorithms Utilized in SAP Solutions

SAP leverages various machine learning algorithms based on specific use cases.

Algorithm

Use Case

Performance

Regression

Forecasting

Moderate

Clustering

Customer segmentation

High

Neural Networks

Image recognition

Very High

Comparison of Algorithm Performance and Use Cases

Our analysis of SAP’s ML toolkit revealed that:

  • Regression is ideal for linear forecasting (e.g., sales predictions).
  • Clustering excels in grouping customers based on purchasing behavior.
  • Neural Networks outperform other models in complex tasks like fraud detection and image recognition.

Best Practices for Leveraging Machine Learning with SAP

1. Data Preparation and Management

Why It Matters: Garbage in, garbage out. High-quality data is the backbone of successful ML models.

  • Use SAP Data Hub for seamless data integration.
  • Regularly clean and normalize data to improve accuracy.

2. Monitoring and Maintenance of Machine Learning Models

Models degrade over time due to changing business conditions (a concept called model drift).

  • Establish a lifecycle management framework to monitor and retrain models periodically.
  • Use SAP AI Core for automated model deployment and maintenance.

Future Trends in Machine Learning with SAP

The future is bright for machine learning in SAP. Here are some trends to watch:

  1. AI-Powered Process Automation:
    SAP is working on next-gen automation tools to reduce manual workloads further.
  2. Emerging Technologies:
    Tools like SAP Conversational AI are becoming more sophisticated, enabling better natural language understanding.
  3. Edge Computing:
    SAP’s focus on edge computing will enable faster decision-making at localized points of business activity.

Conclusion

Integrating machine learning with SAP solutions is a journey filled with challenges, but the rewards far outweigh the risks. By addressing data quality issues, investing in the right tools, and following best practices, businesses can harness the power of machine learning to revolutionize their operations.

From our team’s perspective, the combination of SAP S/4HANA and machine learning is like having a supercharged engine for business efficiency. Dive in, address the challenges, and let innovation lead the way!

FAQs

  1. What are the main benefits of integrating machine learning with SAP?
    Machine learning enhances decision-making, automates tasks, improves customer experiences, and reduces operational costs.
  2. How does SAP S/4HANA support machine learning?
    SAP S/4HANA includes embedded machine learning capabilities for predictive analytics, sentiment analysis, and demand forecasting.
  3. What tools help ensure data quality for ML in SAP?
    Tools like SAP Data Intelligence and SAP Data Hub streamline data integration and cleansing for better ML outcomes.
  4. What industries benefit most from SAP’s ML capabilities?
    Retail, manufacturing, finance, and healthcare are some of the key sectors leveraging SAP’s machine learning tools.
  5. How can businesses overcome skill gaps in ML implementation?
    Invest in training programs through SAP’s online resources or partner with experienced consultants to upskill your team.
  6. What is model drift, and how can it be addressed in SAP?
    Model drift occurs when ML models lose accuracy over time due to changes in data patterns. Regular monitoring and retraining can mitigate this issue.
  7. What are some emerging trends in ML with SAP? AI-powered process automation, conversational AI, and edge computing are shaping the future of ML in SAP solutions.

entry.title: Re: SAP Callidus
entry.content:
Your understanding is mostly correct! "SAP Callidus" is not a single product but rather a suite of products that SAP acquired from CallidusCloud in 2018.

Clarification on Terminology:

  1. SAP Callidus = SAP Sales Performance Management (SPM) Suite

    • When people say "SAP Callidus", they usually mean the entire suite of sales performance and incentive management solutions that came from CallidusCloud.
    • However, some people incorrectly use "SAP Callidus" specifically to refer to SAP Commissions, since that is the most widely used product.

  2. Product Name Mapping (Before & After SAP Acquisition):

    CallidusCloud Product (Before SAP)                                      SAP Rebranded Name (After Acquisition)
    Callidus TrueComp / CallidusCloud CommissionsSAP Commissions
    Callidus TrueProducer / CallidusCloud Producer ProSAP Agent Lifecycle Management (SAP ALM)
    Callidus CPQSAP CPQ (Configure, Price, Quote)
    Callidus Territory and QuotaSAP Territory and Quota
    Callidus Litmos (LMS)SAP Litmos (Later sold off, no longer part of SAP)

  3. SAP’s Official Position:

    • SAP does not use the name "SAP Callidus" officially anymore.
    • They refer to it as SAP Sales Cloud or SAP Sales Performance Management (SPM).
    • However, many professionals in the industry still say "SAP Callidus" when they mean SAP Commissions.

Final Answer:

  • "SAP Callidus" originally referred to a suite of products from CallidusCloud.
  • Some people now incorrectly use it to refer only to SAP Commissions.
  • SAP officially refers to these products under SAP Sales Performance Management (SPM).

entry.title: Analytic Model was created with older version of the editor
entry.content:
After last update of SAP Datasphere one of our Analytic Model is break

We receive the message : Analytic Model was created with older version of the editor. Delete and recreate the Analytic Model.

It's impossible to edit the Analytic Model, we can't restore a copy of our JSON file exported before the transport in production

After investigation, we think it is caused due special characters in the technical name

When we try to redeploy the model, we see the message below in the log.

Review the following warnings: Column(s) (/BIC/C_AIE_143) of entity "SPACE001.VG_ISU_DEVICE" contains one or more of (/, \, ", .) character(s) and the same are replaced in the analytical model.

Some fields that are used start by  /BIC/.. these fields are come from BW infoObject or ADSO and worked fine before the update.

Only one solution rebuilt a big Analytic Model 😞

It is therefore noticed that SAP unilaterally changes the functioning of its application without taking into account that there are customers behind the application.

entry.title: Need Help with Error 32 - Broken Pipe, HTTP Adapter - Avoid Sending Response in Certain Cases
entry.content:
Hi Tech Community,

I’m facing the following issue:

A sender using HTTP (which, as we know, is synchronous) sends a payload and expects a response. If the flow crashes, there is no business impact since the sender has a retry mechanism.

The sender side closes the connection after 30 seconds due to business requirements. However, we cannot catch this error because it is not raised during the flow execution but rather occurs outside of it (if I understand correctly how servlets are built). As a result, while the business is not impacted, CPI monitoring detects failures and requires constant checks.

This results in Error 32 - Broken Pipe, as the connection is already closed when the response is attempted to be sent back.

I attempted to implement logic that terminates the flow after 30+ seconds, but the Termination End still sends a response to the sender, causing a failure due to the closed connection.

Does anyone have insights on this issue or ideas on how to prevent sending a response with the HTTP adapter in certain cases?

Looking forward to your responses.

Best regards,
Andrejs

entry.title: Re: Controlling internal movement of an expired Batch and blocked stock type- BAdI /SCWM/EX_WHO_DSTG
entry.content:
Hi Shailesh,

1) Is it possible to store items with mixed expiry dates in the same bin?  I've noticed the MIX SLED option in the Storage Type configuration, but it appears that different expiry dates cannot be managed as they are either set to the earliest date, the latest date, or not allowed altogether.   

If so, how can this be achieved?                                                                                                                                                    

2) We have multiple serial numbers with different expiration dates. We want to change those dates if they are usable in the future, but we are encountering difficulties.

While there is an option in the warehouse monitor to change attributes in physical stock, it is not effective for serials with different expiration dates within the same bin. This behavior seems to be influenced by the MIX SLED configuration in the storage type.

Could you please advise on how we can change the expiration dates in this scenario?

Thank you in advance.

Regards,

Sankara

EWM - Interfaces  EWM - Shipping and Receiving  EWM - Warehouse Order Processing

entry.title: Bad Request Error while creating SAP BTP Process Integration Runtime instance using Terraform
entry.content:
My Goal - To create and maintain SAP BTP infrastructure like Directory, Subaccounts, Cloud Foundry subscription, Process Integration Runtime instance, Role Collections, Roles, etc for transformation projects using Terraform

What I have accomplished so far - I have managed to use Terraform to create Directory, Subaccounts, Cloud Foundry subscription and assign Process Integration Runtime entitlement to the subaccount, but while creating an instance, its throwing a Bad Request error.

The Code - 

 

data "btp_subaccount_service_plan" "pir_service_plan" {
  subaccount_id = var.subaccount_id
  name          = btp_subaccount_entitlement.pir_entl.service_name
  offering_name = btp_subaccount_entitlement.pir_entl.plan_name
}
 
resource "btp_subaccount_service_instance" "pir" {
  subaccount_id = var.subaccount_id  
  # The service plan ID can be looked up via the data source btp_subaccount_service_plan
  serviceplan_id = <<This ID is what I need from the previous Data resource>>
  name           = "pir-${var.subaccount_id}"

  parameters = jsonencode({
"roles": [
       <<Role1>>,
       <Role2>>
    ],
    "grant-types": [
        "client_credentials"
    ],
    "redirect-uris": [],
    "token-validity": 43200

  })
}

 

 

Essentially, I am trying to use the resource "btp_subaccount_service_instance" to create an instance of Process Integration Runtime. But for this I need the serviceplan_Id, which I should be able to get using the data source "btp_subaccount_service_plan". 

 

But no matter what data I pass to this data source I get the following error - 

Planning failed. Terraform encountered an error while generating this plan.

Error: API Error Reading Resource Service Plan (Subaccount) with module.process_integration_runtime.data.btp_subaccount_service_plan.service_plans, on modules/process_integration_runtime/process_integration_runtime.tf line 59, in data "btp_subaccount_service_plan" "service_ 59: data "btp_subaccount_service_plan" "service_plans" { │ │ BadRequest ╵ Operation failed: failed running terraform plan (exit 1) 

Currently, I am passing "it-rt" as the name parameter and "api" as the offering_name parameter. No matter what value I give, it's always throwing me an error.  And the error description isn't detailed enough to debug!

Any help is greatly appreciated

Thanks

Kriba

entry.title: Re: Not able to add more than one data source in sapui5 a...
entry.content:
I have identified the solution. To resolve the issue, please follow these steps:

  1. Open Application Info.

  2. Navigate to Manage Service Models.

  3. Select the appropriate destination.

  4. Click Refresh & Save.

This process will fix the issue.

Note: You must stop and restart the preview to see the service changes.

entry.title: Fiori & CAP: getting custom action working
entry.content:
Hi,

In a list report on the detail page (object page) , I'm not understanding in general how custom action bound to an entity is to be implemented. I like to update table Content on an object page with an action related to the current displayed entity which is bound to the object page.

The Fiori sdk docu is confusing, the flexible programming odel homepage with section "custom Actions" is not really helpful, does not explain in comments what should be done.

My main entity is SystemKinds, my table on the object page is SystemKinds.texts . I simply like to add a custom action in the toolbar of table SystemKinds.Texts. Because of having bound the object page to SystemKinds, I assume I have to use "bound action". Once the button is pressed, "title" value should be entered by the user.

I also know that I need side effect once the button on a new action is pressed in order to get values in the SystemKinds.texts updated.

Is there a sample which I can check? In the Fiori Show case app, I cannot find my case.
I need the Fiori annotation for the UI and manifest Setup. I tried different things but nothing works.
I need a short advice in Fiori Setup (annotations with UI) . The action is "setSystemKindsTitles".

Backend Looks like:

 

 

 entity SystemKinds as projection on paltr.BaseSystemKinds
    actions {      
      @(Common.SideEffects: {TargetEntities: ['in/texts']})
      action setSystemKindsTitles(
                                  @(                                   UI.Placeholder:'{i18n>Paltr_Common_InputTitlePlaceHolder}',
                                    title:'{i18n>Paltr_Common_InputTitle}'
                                  )
                                  title : String);


    };

  // --- SystemKinds.texts ---
  @Capabilities: {
    Insertable            : false,
    Deletable             : false,
    Updatable,
    NavigationRestrictions: {Navigability: #None, }
  }
  entity SystemKinds.texts as projection on paltr.BaseSystemKinds.texts;

 

 

In my Service,I have implemented:

 

 

...
class AdminConfigurationService extends cds.ApplicationService {

   const { SystemTypes, SystemKinds } = this.entities;

if (SystemKinds.texts) {
      this.on('setSystemKindsTitles', SystemKinds, async (req) => {
        if (!!SystemKinds.texts?.drafts) {
          await this.update(SystemKinds.texts.drafts, req.data.ID).with({ title: req.data.title });
        }

      });
    }
   ...
}

 

 

My currently used Fiori UI looks like the following:

 

 

using AdminConfigurationService as service from '../../srv/paltr.service.core.adminconfiguration';

annotate service.SystemKinds with @(
    // Listreport SystemKindList

    UI.LineItem                                          : [
        {
            $Type: 'UI.DataField',
            Value: title,
        },
        {
            $Type: 'UI.DataField',
            Value: isPreset,
        },
        {
            $Type: 'UI.DataField',
            Value: isUsable,
        },
    ],

    // SystemKindObjectPage
    UI.HeaderInfo                                        : {
        TypeName      : '{i18n>Paltr_SystemKind}',
        TypeNamePlural: '{i18n>Paltr_SystemKinds}',
        Title         : {
            $Type            : 'UI.DataField',
            Value            : techName,
            ![@UI.Importance]: #High,
            ![@UI.Hidden]    : false,

        },


    },

    // SystemKindObjectPage - section General Information
    UI.FieldGroup #Paltr_GeneralInformationFieldGroup    : {
        $Type: 'UI.FieldGroupType',
        Data : [
            {
                $Type: 'UI.DataField',
                Value: techName,
            },
            {
                $Type: 'UI.DataField',
                Value: ID,
            },
            {
                $Type : 'UI.DataFieldForAnnotation',
                Target: '@UI.ConnectedFields#Paltr_GeneralInformationConnected',
                Label : '{i18n>Paltr_LastChange}',
            },
            {
                $Type: 'UI.DataField',
                Value: isPreset,
            },
        ],
    },
    UI.ConnectedFields #Paltr_GeneralInformationConnected: {
        $Type   : 'UI.ConnectedFieldsType',
        Template: '{modifiedBy}{modifiedAt}',
        Data    : {
            $Type     : 'Core.Dictionary',
            modifiedBy: {
                $Type: 'UI.DataField',
                Value: modifiedBy,
            },
            modifiedAt: {
                $Type: 'UI.DataField',
                Value: modifiedAt,
            },
        },
    },

    // SystemKindObjectPage - section Settings
    UI.FieldGroup #Paltr_SettingsFieldGroup              : {
        $Type: 'UI.FieldGroupType',
        Data : [

        {
            $Type: 'UI.DataField',
            Value: isUsable,
        }],

    },


    UI.Facets                                            : [
        //Section General Information
        {
            $Type : 'UI.ReferenceFacet',
            ID    : 'SystemKind_GeneralInformationFacet',
            Label : '{i18n>Paltr_GeneralInformation}',
            Target: '@UI.FieldGroup#Paltr_GeneralInformationFieldGroup',
        },

        //Section Settings
        {
            $Type : 'UI.CollectionFacet',
            Label : '{i18n>Paltr_BaseData}',
            ID    : 'SystemKind_SettingsFacet',
            Facets: [{
                $Type : 'UI.ReferenceFacet',
                Label : '{i18n>Paltr_Settings}',
                ID    : 'SystemKind_SettingsGroupFacet',
                Target: '@UI.FieldGroup#Paltr_SettingsFieldGroup',
            }],
        },

        // Translations table
        {
            $Type : 'UI.ReferenceFacet',
            Label : '{i18n>Paltr_Translations}',
            ID    : 'Paltr_Translations',
            Target: 'texts/@UI.LineItem#Paltr_Translations',

        },

   
    ],


);

annotate service.SystemKinds.texts with @(UI.LineItem #Paltr_Translations: [
    {
        $Type            : 'UI.DataField',
        Value            : locale,
        Label            : '{i18n>Paltr_Sprache}',
        ![@UI.Importance]: #High,
    },
    {
        $Type            : 'UI.DataField',
        Value            : title,
        ![@UI.Importance]: #High,
    },

    
]);

 

 

The new action shoud appear in the toolbar of "Translations table". I have deleted all my own trials around actions.

Any help would be great. 

Yours

Stephan

entry.title: Post good receipt BADI on Public cloud - Addition of Batch and characteristic in Custom fields
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have a scenario where i need to display some extra fields in the APP- Material document overview. I need to display batch and characteristics details in the item page. The std fields are not available in the adapt UI mode.

We tried to create 2 custom fields for the same and added it in Adapt UI. The fields are coming now but I am not able to fill values into in during the runtime since its all CDS views and SEGW odata service. I dont have any point to fill it during runtime during display of items.

So to fill the two custom fields We tried to implement the BADI -MMIM_MODIFY_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_ITEM  which gets called from Post good receipt app. The user enters the PO and batch and characteristics details in the screen whcih we need to show on the Material document overview APP.

But this BADI does not have any parameters which can read the values from the screen. It only gives header and line item data so we are not able to update the custom field values. This is leading blank value in the report- Material document overview for those custom fields.

 

Is there any badi to achieve this requirement ?

Or is there anything that can be done on the material document overview app to display extra std fields related to batch via extensibility ?

 

Material document overview - Item data

 

 

In Migo user enters the batch details

Doing a select on the CDS does not help since the data is not yet saved.

 

Now the only option left is to create a new Material document overview app with custom CDS views and bring those value as extra columns using logic. But i see this solution as very time consuming for such a small ask.

 

Note : This is to be done on S4 HANA PUBLIC CLOUD.

 

Any other approach/help/pointers/guidance would be appreciated.

entry.title: Re: SAP CDP - How to identify the hostname?
entry.content:
Hi @oliverschultze ,

To ensure the integration works, you first need to create an SAP Sales Cloud and SAP Service Cloud Version 2 tenant, with each tenant representing a business unit in your CDP.
Once the tenants are created, one of the step is to set up the Communication System to enable data exchange with CDP.
When creating the Communication System, use the host name from the URL (e.g., https://cdp.Eu5-prod.gigya.com), the OAuth token URL, and your Client Credentials from the CDP event.
For further details, refer to Communication Systems in the documentation

Thanks,
Anand Raj

entry.title: SAP CPQ Integration with Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM)
entry.content:
Background

The Sirion Contract Lifecycle Management (CLM) sell side adapter for SAP CPQ seamlessly integrates the contracting flow to the sell side process. New quotes created on SAP CPQ are automatically transitioned into a Contract Request on Sirion CLM enabling a sync between two systems for the entire sales and contracting flow.

The package contains:

1. Integration flows for:
    a) Initiation of Contract Draft Requests based on SAP CPQ quotes including line items
    b) Tracking the contract request status and enabling quick access to the Contract on Sirion CLM

2. Configuration guides for SAP CPQ, SAP Cloud Integration, Sirion CLM and Integration flow are available on the Documents tab.

The adapter can be configured by defining integration points, mapping data fields between SAP CPQ and Sirion CLM, setting up Sirion API authentication on SAP Cloud Integration.  

Note : This will be partner based Integration packages offered and supports for SAP CPQ.

Integration Package - SAP API Business Hub

SAP CPQ - CLM Integration Document

else

entry.title: SuccessFactors Saudi Arabia End Of Service Calculator
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

Introduction:

The end-of-service gratuity is considered one of the worker’s rights owed to the employer in the event of the termination of the work contract, and the Saudi legislator obliged the employer to pay it to the worker at the end of the work contract, whether the work contract was of a fixed-term or indefinite period. Therefore, with the upcoming solution I have made it easy for you to calculate the end-of-service benefit, the due date of the benefit, and how to calculate it according to the rules of the Saudi labor system according to the data already stored in the SuccessFactors Employee Central.

The Requirement:

To avoid calculating the EOS out side the system and do it directedly from SuccessFactors as same as the rest of processes and be able to document generation automatically from the system, we need to build a custom MDF (EOS Calculator) to set the required Workflow and generate the EOS documents and avoid any manual entry in ECP system.

The Challenge:

We need a complicated solution to calculate the End Of Service as per the KSA rule and regulations.

The EOS Calculations:                                                                                                         

EOS Basics: 

EOS (For 5 years of service and less) = 0.5 of total salary for each year = (0.5*Salary*Service years)

EOS (For more than 5 years of service period) = (0.5 of One last monthly salary for 5 years) + (after 5 years will be One complete monthly salary for each year only the years after the 5 years) = (0.5*One Monthly Salary*5) + (Last Monthly Salary*remain service years)

Total Monthly Salary = Basic + Housing Allowance+ Trans+ Other Recurring Allowance

Payment Frequency (Number of days in Year) = 365                                                                                                                                                

Calculation - Example: 

Nov 2024 Period                                                                      

Actual Date of hire on 01.05.2017             

Terminated on 30.11.2024                                                    

No of Days 01.05.2017 to 30.11.2024 => 2770                                                            

Gratuity Basis -> Basic + Housing Allowance + Transportation + Other Recurring => 15000 + 3000 + 2000 + 2000 = 22000     

 No of Service year => 2770 / 365 => 6.219  

EOS for 5 years = 0.5*22000*5 = 55000                                               

EOS for 1.219 year = 1.219 * 22000 = 26818

EOS for 6.219 years = 55000 + 26818 = 81818                                        

The Cases:  

In Case of Resignation:

No. Of Years

Calculation

 Deserved EOS

From ZERO to 2 Years

ZERO End of Service

 0

From 2 Years TO 5 Years

1/3 End of Service Amount

81818 * 1/3 = 27272.666

From 5 Years TO 10 Years

2/3 End of Service Amount

81818 * 2/3 = 54545.333

From 10 Years and Above

Full End of Service Amount

81818

In Case of Termination:

Condition

Calculation

End of Employment Reason " Mutual Agreement 74"

Full End Of Service Amount + WT 6044 " Compensation Contract"

End of Employment Reason "Company no Reason 77"

Full End Of Service Amount + Last Basic Salary * WT ZEOS

End of Employments Reason "Labor law 80"

ZERO End Of Service & Only Leave Balance Encashment

Approach:

We will use 2 composite associated MDFs the parent MDF will do the basic calculations then the child MDF will do the rest of calculation and totals of dues, deductions and Net. 

The Solution:

The 2 composite associated MDFs as following:

  1. Finalization of Employee's Account ... The Parent MDF
  2. DUES Calculator ... The Child MDF

Let’s Start with First One the parent object: Finalization of Employee's Account

The custom fields:

  • End of Service Type                                                  
  • Join Date                                                      
  • Service Period  
  • Monthly Earning                                         
  • EOS Indemnity                                             

 The Associations:

Name: Total Dues

Multiplicity: One to One

Destination Object: DUES Calculator

Type: Composite                                                       

The Rules assigned:

1. In the effective start date field

  • EOS Hire Date Rule

  • EOS Monthly earning Rule, Monthly earnings calculated in a custom filed created in the compensation section by dividing the annual pay component group /12

2. In the cust_EOS_Type field

  • Add Evaluate Rule in the cust_EOS_Type field to calculate the service period and show up automatically once you chose the EOS Type as following:

3. In the cust_Service_Period filed

  • Add Evaluate rule to cust_Service_Period filed to calculate the EOS Indemnity as we explained earlier as following:   

4. In cust_EOS field

  • Add Evaluate Rule in cust_EOS to Calculate the Deserved EOS according to the type of termination as follow:

The second MDF the child object: DUES Calculator

The custom fields:

  • End OF Service Deserved
  • Annual Leave
  • Over Time
  • Bonus
  • Additional Amount from the Company
  • Notice Period Salary
  • Compensation Salary as Per Article 77
  • Personal Loan
  • Car Insurance
  • Other Deductions
  • Other Deduction Description
  • TOTAL DUES
  • TOTAL DEDUCTIONS
  • NET TO BE PAID / RECOVERED                               

The Rules assigned:

1. Add a Save Rule: To Calculate the total dues, total deductions and Net

2. In the EOS Deserved Field:

  • Add Evaluate Rule to Set amount with zero to make all custom added fields equal zero by default

 The EOS Calculator on the employee profile:

The end Result is an EOS calculator on the employee profile

Document Generation:

You Can build the document generation form to match the customer theme for example see the below:

Hope this help you in your KSA system configurations 

Thank you 

Ahmed Aranda

entry.title: Blog-Understanding the Types of Purchase Requisitions in SAP MM module
entry.content:
Understanding the Types of Purchase Requisitions in SAP MM

In SAP Materials Management (MM), the purchase requisition is a critical document in the procurement process. It is essentially an internal request to procure goods or services, initiated by users within an organization, which is then reviewed, approved, and processed by the purchasing department. The type of purchase requisition determines how it is processed within SAP MM, and understanding the different types is crucial for effective procurement management. In this blog, we’ll dive into the various types of purchase requisitions in SAP MM and when they should be used.

What is a Purchase Requisition?

A Purchase Requisition (PR) in SAP MM is a formal request to the purchasing department to acquire materials or services. It contains information such as material type, quantity, delivery date, and cost center. The purchase requisition is the first step in the procurement cycle and acts as a request for the purchase order (PO), which is ultimately sent to suppliers.

Once a purchase requisition is created, it undergoes approval workflows, and the purchase order is created either manually or automatically depending on configuration settings and system functionality.

Types of Purchase Requisitions in SAP MM

SAP MM offers different types of purchase requisitions, each serving distinct business needs. The classification of these types plays a significant role in defining how the PRs are processed and routed within the system.

1. Standard Purchase Requisition

This is the most common type of purchase requisition used for regular procurement needs. It is created manually by users who require goods or services. Typically, this PR is associated with a specific material or service, and users can specify details like quantity, delivery date, and account assignment.

Use Case:

  • When a user needs to procure standard items like office supplies, raw materials, etc.
  • When a specific purchase order does not yet exist.

Key Features:

  • Can be created for materials and services.
  • Allows manual entry of data such as vendor, material group, or special instructions.
  • Used for both internal and external procurement.

2. Consignment Purchase Requisition

A consignment purchase requisition is used when materials are delivered to the company but remain the property of the supplier until consumed. This type of purchase requisition is typically used for stock that is held in consignment inventory, meaning the organization doesn't own it until it is used.

Use Case:

  • When materials are received but ownership remains with the vendor (e.g., spare parts, raw materials).
  • Frequently used in long-term supplier relationships.

Key Features:

  • Does not trigger a purchase order right away.
  • Used for materials held in consignment stock.
  • Helps track stock consumption for eventual billing to the company.

3. Subcontracting Purchase Requisition

Subcontracting PRs are created when the company needs to send raw materials or components to a third-party vendor for further processing, manufacturing, or assembly. The subcontractor performs the work and then returns the finished goods to the organization. This process is managed through a subcontracting PR in SAP.

Use Case:

  • When outsourcing certain manufacturing processes or when specific components need additional processing.
  • Often used in industries like electronics, automotive, and textiles.

Key Features:

  • Includes both the raw materials being sent and the service required for processing.
  • Allows tracking of both goods and services in the procurement process.
  • Requires close monitoring of the work done by the subcontractor.

4. Stock Transfer Purchase Requisition

This type of PR is used when goods are being transferred from one plant to another within the same organization. In SAP, stock transfer requisitions are used to move stock between storage locations or plants, without involving external vendors.

Use Case:

  • When inventory needs to be moved between different plants or warehouses.
  • Frequently used in multi-plant organizations for stock redistribution.

Key Features:

  • Does not involve external vendors or suppliers.
  • Involves intercompany stock movements.
  • Requires no purchase order as no external purchase is involved.

5. Framework Agreement Purchase Requisition

Framework agreements are long-term procurement contracts between an organization and a vendor. Purchase requisitions created against these agreements are typically tied to specific terms such as pricing, quantity, and delivery dates over an extended period.

Use Case:

  • When the company has an ongoing contract with a supplier for repeated purchases over time.
  • Suitable for regular or bulk procurement needs.

Key Features:

  • Can be tied to Quantity Contracts or Value Contracts.
  • Reduces the need to create individual purchase requisitions or purchase orders for each order.
  • Helps streamline the purchasing process by enabling flexible and long-term procurement.

6. Services Purchase Requisition

A services PR is used when an organization requires services rather than tangible goods. This PR type is specifically created for services such as maintenance work, consulting, or any other service-related procurement.

Use Case:

  • When the company needs to procure external services (e.g., cleaning, IT services, consultancy).
  • Suitable for non-material procurement.

Key Features:

  • Does not involve material, but rather a specific service.
  • Allows for tracking service specifications, work descriptions, and service terms.
  • Typically linked to a purchase order for services (PO for services).

7. Internal Purchase Requisition (Non-Material Purchase)

This PR type is used when the request is for non-material goods, such as services or consumables that don't need to be tracked in inventory. These PRs are often used for internal purposes like spare parts, office supplies, or administrative items.

Use Case:

  • When non-material items like consumables or services are needed.
  • Common in administrative departments or for internal services.

Key Features:

  • Allows creation of requisitions for items that are not stored as inventory.
  • Flexible in terms of how goods or services are categorized.

Conclusion

In SAP MM, purchase requisitions are vital for streamlining the procurement process. By understanding the different types of purchase requisitions, businesses can tailor their processes to fit their specific procurement needs. Whether it’s for standard materials, subcontracting, or long-term agreements, each PR type ensures the right goods and services are sourced in the most efficient manner.

As businesses grow and procurement becomes more complex, utilizing the right type of purchase requisition can help increase operational efficiency, reduce procurement cycle times, and improve supplier relationships. Configuring and managing PRs correctly in SAP MM is therefore key to ensuring a smooth and responsive procurement process.

Let us see what the future will bring

C.B.

entry.title: Handling Cash Advances with SAP Concur's Financial Integration Service v4 API's
entry.content:
I'm a long-time Technical Consultant at SAP Concur, specializing in data interfaces between SAP Concur and its customers. In this article, I'll walk you through using SAP Concur's Financial Integration Service (FIS) v4 Web Service API to manage the issuance and repayment of cash advances.

Prerequisites

Before diving in, it's essential to have a basic understanding of the FIS API. If you're not familiar, I highly recommend reading this blog post.   You'll also need a Concur Web application with the following scope:

  • FISVC

Introduction

Cash advances can be incredibly useful for travelers. Here’s why:

  • Convenience: They provide quick access to local currency, especially when you're short on cash or in a place without accessible banking facilities. This feature is a lifesaver in emergencies or remote locations.
  • Reduced Need to Carry Large Amounts of Cash: Carrying less cash reduces the risk of loss or theft, enhancing your personal safety.
  • Emergency Situations: A cash advance can act as a financial lifeline when unexpected expenses arise, such as medical emergencies or hotel bills.
  • Flexibility: You can withdraw money anytime, regardless of local bank hours, which is highly convenient in countries with different banking schedules.
  • No Need for Prior Currency Exchange: Avoid the hassle and expense of currency exchange services. Cash advances often secure better exchange rates compared to traditional methods.

However, please keep in mind that cash advances usually come with fees and interest charges, often higher than typical credit card purchases. Use them judiciously to manage your travel budget effectively

How SAP Concur Supports Cash Advances

SAP Concur primarily facilitates cash advances in two ways:

  • Employee-Initiated Requests: Employees can request cash advances before they travel using SAP Concur’s Cash Advance Request feature.  If you would like to see a process flow describing a standard process, click here.
  • Card-Initiated Transactions: Transactions that come directly from your corporate card issuer.

Both methods have financial posting implications and tracking requirements, making the Financial Integration Service APIs invaluable.

Remember, there are 3 steps to Financial Integration Service:

  1. Getting Financial Transactions
  2. Acknowledging each Financial Transaction
  3. Confirming each Financial Transaction (success or fail)

For issuing a cash advance, use doctype=cashadvance. For repayment and tracking, use doctype=expense. 

Detailed Walkthrough

Issuing a Cash Advance

Let's focus on the critical data elements rather than the actual API calls, which are detailed in the original FIS blog post.  When receiving a document:

  • “id”: This is the docId used for acknowledgements and posting confirmations.
  • “employeeData”: Identifies the employee receiving the cash advance.
  • “cardTransactionID”: If provided, helps reconcile the advance to the corporate card.
  • “cashAdvanceId”: Use this field to track the cash advance.
  • “journalData”: Tells you whether to pay the employee or credit the card.

Here's an example of a cash advance taken out on a corporate card:

In the above example, the employee used their CBCP corporate card to take out a cash advance for $26.07. The accounting entries would be to debit the "CASHADV" account and credit the "card clearing account" for 26.07.

For employee-initiated cash advances, the cardTransaction* fields are null and the journalData looks like:

The accounting would debit the "CASHADV from empl" account and credit the employees payable for 1000.00.

Please remember that the cash advance must be acknowledged and posting confirmed successful before the employee can submit expenses against it. Otherwise, it will be in Pending or Failure status and can only be accessed by your Concur Cash Advance Administrator. 

Repayment of a Cash Advance

After the cash advance is confirmed, employees will associate the advance with an expense report and submit expenses against it. These expenses will all be cash/out-of-pocket expenses.

In the new section called "cashAdvanceApplication" within the JSON from the GET call, each cash expenseEntry that was used to repay the advance will be listed.

Here's an example:

In the above JSON, two accounting entries are needed:

  1. From the journal section: debit account "Dinnerx" and credit employee payable for 26.07.
  2. From the cashAdvanceApplication section: debit employee payable and credit "CASHADV".

Concur ensures that the employee repays the advance. Depending on the configuration, repayment can be completed in one or multiple expense reports. More than one advance can be associated with a single expense report, necessitating multiple entries in the cashAdvanceApplication section for each cashAdvanceID..

Conclusion

Cash advances can be complex to manage, but with SAP Concur’s Financial Integration Service, you can issue and track them effortlessly. The near real-time bi-directional interface provides critical feedback for the ERP posting process.

With the insights from this post and the Postman collection from the previous blog, you should be well on your way to exploring the benefits of the Financial Integration Service.

Share and Connect

What do you think?  Do you have anything to add? Leave a comment below.

Did you find it useful? Give us a like and share on social media.

Do you have questions or want to know more about SAP Concur and its offerings? Please follow us here.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: SAP RISE - Key Learnings
entry.content:
Hi @savithakumar ,

this is an excellent blog, thank you for taking the time to write it and share your lessons learned from the RISE DMO migration.

For anybody on the RISE journey and doing the DMO migration or planning it, detailed factual engine room insights like these lessons learned are extremely useful.

Please don't stop here, the SAP Community and the SAP world in general needs more blogs like this, so, keep blogging what you are doing with RISE and ECS.

By the way, I've added a link to your blog in the https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/%EF%B8%8F-the-sap-s-4hana-rise-amp-sap-btp-toolbox-%EF%B8%8F/ba-p/13944069 SAP S/4HANA RISE & BTP Toolbox, in the lessons learned section.

All the best,

Andy Silvey.

p.s. out of curiosity, some questions...

. Was it DMO S/4 OnPremise to S/4 on RISE, or was it DMO ECC to S/4 RISE ?

. How many gb was the Data Footprint before the migration and afterwards ?

. During the DMO, what was the Data transfer throughput rate, from OnPrem to ECS ? How many mb or gb per hour did you get when transferring the data from your Data Center to RISE/ECS ?

. Did you do any special preparation or tuning to get a higher Data transfer throughput rate ?

. What S/4HANA did you build on RISE/ECS, is it with HA and DR ?

With the answers to these questions, you could make that into your next blog 🙂

entry.title: Re: Smooth transition to ABAP for Cloud Development(Cheat sheet)
entry.content:
Thanks a lot. Here's a little update about "Job scheduling":

  • The "Step 3" indicates to create the Application Job Catalog Entry and the Application Job Template by a custom program, using the API "CL_APJ_DT_CREATE_CONTENT", but you can now fully use the form-based Eclipse ADT editors to create them. These editors used to exist since 2022 but were incomplete until version 2411.
  • Also, note that the new interfaces IF_APJ_RT_RUN and IF_APJ_DT_DEFAULTS can be used since the version 2411 for the "Class with Execute Method", which supersede the "old" interfaces IF_APJ_DT_EXEC_OBJECT and IF_APJ_RT_EXEC_OBJECT.

entry.title: Re: Using "onAfterDataEntryProcess" event to implement Default Logic
entry.content:
@N1kh1l 

Hi Nikhil
If your data actions have *FOREACH loops that reference Product then I would definitely scope on Entity and Product. You can test different versions of the data actions scoping:
     Entity only
     Entity + Product
before implementing any scripting and measure performance with larger parameters selections.

From my experience, Planning is a very iterative process. I would expect users to make a number of limited changes and then want to see the impact. This would potentially limit the size of the parameter lists being passed into the data action.

regards, Chris

entry.title: API to create Application Job Catalog Entry & Template, before CL_APJ_DT_CREATE_CONTENT
entry.content:
In this blog post, I propose the code of a demo program to create Application Job Catalog Entries and Templates, which works in ABAP 7.52 SP04. In this version, the released API "CL_APJ_DT_CREATE_CONTENT" cannot be used as it doesn't exist. There are lots of things missing in this demo program, like error management. To be used at your own risk.

One word about the Application Job framework: it's the new way to schedule background jobs. It exists since ABAP 7.50, and it's still used and developed in ABAP Cloud. The old job framework (JOB_OPEN, etc.) cannot be used with ABAP Cloud, so I recommend reading more about the Application Jobs, for instance here: Scheduling Application Jobs in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Application Jobs in BTP, ABAP Environment | SAP Help Portal, Working with Application Job Objects | SAP Help Portal. Also, this external blog post series is well written and short: BTP - Application Jobs (Introduction), BTP - Application Job (Creation), BTP - Application Job (v2).

Here's the demo code below. Again, at your own risk!

Thanks for reading.

Sandra

 

REPORT zdemo_create_app_job_objects.

CLASS lcl_apj_helper DEFINITION FINAL
  CREATE PRIVATE.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    "! Call TR_TASK_SET like does the method SET_INTERNAL of ZCL_ABAPGIT_DEFAULT_TRANSPORT.
    CLASS-METHODS set_default_transport
      IMPORTING trkorr TYPE trkorr.

    "! Call TR_TADIR_INTERFACE like does the method tadir_insert of ZCL_ABAPGIT_OBJECTS_SUPER.
    CLASS-METHODS write_tadir_entry
      IMPORTING !name             TYPE apj_w_jce_root-job_catalog_entry_name
                tadir_object_type TYPE trobjtype
                devclass          TYPE devclass.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS lcl_app DEFINITION FINAL
  CREATE PRIVATE.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    CLASS-METHODS create
      RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE REF TO lcl_app.

    METHODS create_or_change_catalog_entry.
    METHODS create_or_change_template.
    METHODS delete_catalog_entry.
    METHODS delete_template.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS lcl_jce DEFINITION FINAL
  CREATE PRIVATE.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    DATA data_accessor TYPE REF TO cl_apj_dt_job_cat_entry READ-ONLY.

    CLASS-METHODS create
      IMPORTING !name         TYPE apj_w_jce_root-job_catalog_entry_name
      RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE REF TO lcl_jce.

    CLASS-METHODS delete
      IMPORTING !name  TYPE apj_w_jce_root-job_catalog_entry_name
                trkorr TYPE trkorr.

    CLASS-METHODS get
      IMPORTING !name         TYPE apj_w_jce_root-job_catalog_entry_name
      RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE REF TO lcl_jce.

    CLASS-METHODS exists
      IMPORTING !name         TYPE apj_w_jce_root-job_catalog_entry_name
      RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE abap_bool.

    METHODS save_and_activate
      IMPORTING devclass TYPE devclass
                trkorr   TYPE trkorr.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    CONSTANTS entity_type TYPE apj_s_dt_dba-entity_type VALUE if_apj_dt_types=>cs_entity_type-job_catalog_entry.

    DATA name TYPE apj_w_jce_root-job_catalog_entry_name.
    DATA mode TYPE if_apj_dt_types=>ty_mode.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS lcl_jt DEFINITION FINAL
  CREATE PRIVATE.

  PUBLIC SECTION.
    DATA data_accessor TYPE REF TO cl_apj_dt_job_template_entry READ-ONLY.

    CLASS-METHODS create
      IMPORTING !name         TYPE apj_w_jt_root-job_template_name
      RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE REF TO lcl_jt.

    CLASS-METHODS delete
      IMPORTING !name  TYPE apj_w_jt_root-job_template_name
                trkorr TYPE trkorr.

    CLASS-METHODS get
      IMPORTING !name         TYPE apj_w_jt_root-job_template_name
      RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE REF TO lcl_jt.

    CLASS-METHODS exists
      IMPORTING !name         TYPE apj_w_jt_root-job_template_name
      RETURNING VALUE(result) TYPE abap_bool.

    METHODS save_and_activate
      IMPORTING devclass TYPE devclass
                trkorr   TYPE trkorr.

  PRIVATE SECTION.
    CONSTANTS entity_type TYPE apj_s_dt_dba-entity_type VALUE if_apj_dt_types=>cs_entity_type-job_template.

    DATA name TYPE apj_w_jt_root-job_template_name.
    DATA mode TYPE if_apj_dt_types=>ty_mode.

ENDCLASS.


START-OF-SELECTION.
  lcl_app=>create( )->create_or_change_catalog_entry( ).
*  lcl_app=>create( )->create_or_change_template( ).
*  lcl_app=>create( )->delete_template( ).
*  lcl_app=>create( )->delete_catalog_entry( ).
  ASSERT 1 = 1.


CLASS lcl_apj_helper IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD set_default_transport.
    CALL FUNCTION 'TR_TASK_SET'
      EXPORTING  iv_order          = trkorr
                 iv_validdays      = 1
      EXCEPTIONS invalid_username  = 1
                 invalid_category  = 2
                 invalid_client    = 3
                 invalid_validdays = 4
                 invalid_order     = 5
                 invalid_task      = 6
                 OTHERS            = 7.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      " TODO
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD write_tadir_entry.
    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_params,
             wi_test_modus       TYPE trpari-s_checked,
             wi_tadir_pgmid      TYPE tadir-pgmid,
             wi_tadir_object     TYPE tadir-object,
             wi_tadir_obj_name   TYPE tadir-obj_name,
             wi_tadir_author     TYPE tadir-author,
             wi_tadir_devclass   TYPE tadir-devclass,
             wi_tadir_masterlang TYPE tadir-masterlang,
             iv_delflag          TYPE tadir-delflag,
           END OF ty_params.

    DATA(params) = VALUE ty_params( wi_test_modus       = abap_false
                                    wi_tadir_pgmid      = 'R3TR'
                                    wi_tadir_object     = tadir_object_type
                                    wi_tadir_obj_name   = name
                                    wi_tadir_author     = sy-uname
                                    wi_tadir_devclass   = devclass
                                    wi_tadir_masterlang = sy-langu
                                    iv_delflag          = abap_false ).
    CALL FUNCTION 'TR_TADIR_INTERFACE'
      EXPORTING  wi_test_modus                  = params-wi_test_modus
                 wi_tadir_pgmid                 = params-wi_tadir_pgmid
                 wi_tadir_object                = params-wi_tadir_object
                 wi_tadir_obj_name              = params-wi_tadir_obj_name
                 wi_tadir_author                = params-wi_tadir_author
                 wi_tadir_devclass              = params-wi_tadir_devclass
                 wi_tadir_masterlang            = params-wi_tadir_masterlang
                 iv_delflag                     = params-iv_delflag
      EXCEPTIONS tadir_entry_not_existing       = 1
                 tadir_entry_ill_type           = 2
                 no_systemname                  = 3
                 no_systemtype                  = 4
                 original_system_conflict       = 5
                 object_reserved_for_devclass   = 6
                 object_exists_global           = 7
                 object_exists_local            = 8
                 object_is_distributed          = 9
                 obj_specification_not_unique   = 10
                 no_authorization_to_delete     = 11
                 devclass_not_existing          = 12
                 simultanious_set_remove_repair = 13
                 order_missing                  = 14
                 no_modification_of_head_syst   = 15
                 pgmid_object_not_allowed       = 16
                 masterlanguage_not_specified   = 17
                 devclass_not_specified         = 18
                 specify_owner_unique           = 19
                 loc_priv_objs_no_repair        = 20
                 gtadir_not_reached             = 21
                 object_locked_for_order        = 22
                 change_of_class_not_allowed    = 23
                 no_change_from_sap_to_tmp      = 24
                 OTHERS                         = 25.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      " TODO
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS lcl_app IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD create.
    result = NEW lcl_app( ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD create_or_change_catalog_entry.
    TYPES ty_jce TYPE REF TO lcl_jce.

    DATA(jce_name) = EXACT apj_w_jce_root-job_catalog_entry_name( 'ZZSRO' ).
    DATA(devclass) = EXACT devclass( 'ZZSRO' ).
    DATA(trkorr) = EXACT trkorr( 'NPLK900082' ).

    DATA(jce) = COND ty_jce( WHEN NOT lcl_jce=>exists( jce_name )
                             THEN lcl_jce=>create( jce_name )
                             ELSE lcl_jce=>get( jce_name ) ).

    DATA(jce_basic_information) = jce->data_accessor->get_root( ).
    DATA(jce_basic_information_update) = VALUE cl_apj_dt_job_cat_entry=>ty_set_root(
                                                   BASE CORRESPONDING #( jce_basic_information )
                                                   report_name = sy-repid
                                                   text        = EXACT #( |{ sy-datum } - { sy-uzeit }| ) ).
    jce->data_accessor->set_root( jce_basic_information_update ).

    jce->save_and_activate( devclass = devclass
                            trkorr   = trkorr ).
    COMMIT WORK.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD create_or_change_template.
    TYPES ty_jt TYPE REF TO lcl_jt.

    DATA(jt_name) = EXACT apj_w_jt_root-job_template_name( 'ZZSRO_TEMPL' ).
    DATA(jce_name) = EXACT apj_w_jce_root-job_catalog_entry_name( 'ZZSRO' ).
    DATA(devclass) = EXACT devclass( 'ZZSRO' ).
    DATA(trkorr) = EXACT trkorr( 'NPLK900082' ).

    DATA(jt) = COND ty_jt( WHEN NOT lcl_jt=>exists( jt_name )
                           THEN lcl_jt=>create( jt_name )
                           ELSE lcl_jt=>get( jt_name ) ).

    DATA(jt_basic_information) = jt->data_accessor->get_root( ).
    " TODO: variable is assigned but never used (ABAP cleaner)
    DATA(jt_values) = jt->data_accessor->get_values( ).

    jt_basic_information-job_catalog_entry_name = jce_name.
    jt_basic_information-text                   = EXACT #( |{ sy-datum } - { sy-uzeit }| ).
    jt->data_accessor->set_root( jt_basic_information ).

    jt->save_and_activate( devclass = devclass
                           trkorr   = trkorr ).
    COMMIT WORK.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD delete_catalog_entry.
    TYPES ty_jce TYPE REF TO lcl_jce.

    DATA(jce_name) = EXACT apj_w_jt_root-job_catalog_entry_name( 'ZZSRO' ).
    DATA(trkorr) = EXACT trkorr( 'NPLK900082' ).

    lcl_jce=>delete( name   = jce_name
                     trkorr = trkorr ).
    COMMIT WORK.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD delete_template.
    TYPES ty_jt TYPE REF TO lcl_jt.

    DATA(jt_name) = EXACT apj_w_jt_root-job_template_name( 'ZZSRO_TEMPL' ).
    DATA(trkorr) = EXACT trkorr( 'NPLK900082' ).

    lcl_jt=>delete( name   = jt_name
                    trkorr = trkorr ).
    COMMIT WORK.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS lcl_jce IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD create.
    IF exists( name ).
      " TODO raise exception
    ENDIF.
    result = NEW lcl_jce( ).
    result->name          = name.
    result->mode          = if_apj_dt_types=>cs_mode-create.
    result->data_accessor = NEW cl_apj_dt_job_cat_entry( is_root = VALUE #( job_catalog_entry_name = name ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD delete.
    IF NOT exists( name ).
      " TODO raise exception
    ENDIF.

    IF trkorr IS NOT INITIAL.
      lcl_apj_helper=>set_default_transport( trkorr ).
    ENDIF.

    DATA(lo_controller) = cl_apj_dt_controller=>get_instance( ).
    lo_controller->delete_single( VALUE #( entity_type    = entity_type
                                           entity_name    = name
                                           entity_version = if_apj_dt_types=>cs_entity_version-active ) ).

    " TODO: variable is assigned but never used (ABAP cleaner)
    DATA(is_success) = lo_controller->save( ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD exists.
    DATA(jce_dt_db_access) = cl_apj_dt_db_access_factory=>get_instance( )->get_db_access( entity_type ).
    DATA(active_jce_existence_info) = jce_dt_db_access->exists(
        it_name_version = VALUE #( ( entity_name    = name
                                     entity_version = if_apj_dt_types=>cs_entity_version-active ) ) ).
    DATA(active_jce_exists) = xsdbool( line_exists( active_jce_existence_info[ KEY entity_name
                                                                               entity_name = name
                                                                               exists      = abap_true ] ) ).
    IF active_jce_exists = abap_true.
      result = abap_true.
    ELSE.
      DATA(inactive_jce_existence_info) = jce_dt_db_access->exists(
          it_name_version = VALUE #( ( entity_name    = name
                                       entity_version = if_apj_dt_types=>cs_entity_version-inactive ) ) ).
      DATA(inactive_jce_exists) = xsdbool( line_exists( inactive_jce_existence_info[ KEY entity_name
                                                                                     entity_name = name
                                                                                     exists      = abap_true ] ) ).
      IF inactive_jce_exists = abap_true.
        result = abap_true.
      ELSE.
        result = abap_false.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get.
    IF NOT exists( name ).
      " TODO raise exception
    ENDIF.
    DATA(lo_controller) = cl_apj_dt_controller=>get_instance( ).
    DATA(li_entity) = lo_controller->if_apj_dt_controller~retrieve_single(
                          is_entity_type_name = VALUE #( entity_type    = entity_type
                                                         entity_name    = name
                                                         entity_version = 0 )
                          iv_edit_mode        = if_apj_dt_types=>cs_edit_mode-exclusive ).
    ASSERT li_entity IS BOUND.

    result = NEW lcl_jce( ).
    result->name          = name.
    result->mode          = if_apj_dt_types=>cs_mode-update.
    result->data_accessor = CAST #( li_entity ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD save_and_activate.
    lcl_apj_helper=>write_tadir_entry( name              = name
                                       tadir_object_type = 'SAJC'
                                       devclass          = devclass ).

    IF trkorr IS NOT INITIAL.
      lcl_apj_helper=>set_default_transport( trkorr ).
    ENDIF.

    IF mode = if_apj_dt_types=>cs_mode-create.
      DATA(object_data) = NEW cl_apj_dt_gui_cat( ).
      object_data->if_wb_object_data_model~generate_from_key( EXACT #( name ) ).

      DATA(persist) = NEW cl_apj_wb_jce_persist( ).
      persist->if_wb_object_persist~save( p_object_data = object_data
                                          p_access_mode = if_wb_adt_rest_resource_co=>co_access_mode_insert ).
    ENDIF.

    DATA(lo_controller) = cl_apj_dt_controller=>get_instance( ).
    lo_controller->modify_single( is_modify = VALUE #( mode       = if_apj_dt_types=>cs_mode-update
                                                       entity_ref = data_accessor ) ).

    " Save and activate
    " TODO: variable is assigned but never used (ABAP cleaner)
    DATA(is_success) = lo_controller->save( ).
    lo_controller->activate( it_entity_type_and_name = VALUE #( ( entity_type = entity_type
                                                                  entity_name = name ) ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS lcl_jt IMPLEMENTATION.
  METHOD create.
    IF exists( name ).
      " TODO raise exception
    ENDIF.
    result = NEW lcl_jt( ).
    result->name          = name.
    result->mode          = if_apj_dt_types=>cs_mode-create.
    result->data_accessor = NEW cl_apj_dt_job_template_entry( is_root = VALUE #( job_template_name = name ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD delete.
    IF NOT exists( name ).
      " TODO raise exception
    ENDIF.

    IF trkorr IS NOT INITIAL.
      lcl_apj_helper=>set_default_transport( trkorr ).
    ENDIF.

    DATA(lo_controller) = cl_apj_dt_controller=>get_instance( ).
    lo_controller->delete_single( VALUE #( entity_type    = entity_type
                                           entity_name    = name
                                           entity_version = if_apj_dt_types=>cs_entity_version-active ) ).
    " TODO: variable is assigned but never used (ABAP cleaner)
    DATA(is_success) = lo_controller->save( ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD exists.
    DATA(jt_dt_db_access) = cl_apj_dt_db_access_factory=>get_instance( )->get_db_access( entity_type ).
    DATA(active_jt_existence_info) = jt_dt_db_access->exists(
        it_name_version = VALUE #( ( entity_name    = name
                                     entity_version = if_apj_dt_types=>cs_entity_version-active ) ) ).
    DATA(active_jt_exists) = xsdbool( line_exists( active_jt_existence_info[ KEY entity_name
                                                                             entity_name = name
                                                                             exists      = abap_true ] ) ).
    IF active_jt_exists = abap_true.
      result = abap_true.
    ELSE.
      DATA(inactive_jt_existence_info) = jt_dt_db_access->exists(
          it_name_version = VALUE #( ( entity_name    = name
                                       entity_version = if_apj_dt_types=>cs_entity_version-inactive ) ) ).
      DATA(inactive_jt_exists) = xsdbool( line_exists( inactive_jt_existence_info[ KEY entity_name
                                                                                   entity_name = name
                                                                                   exists      = abap_true ] ) ).
      IF inactive_jt_exists = abap_true.
        result = abap_true.
      ELSE.
        result = abap_false.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD get.
    IF NOT exists( name ).
      " TODO raise exception
    ENDIF.
    DATA(lo_controller) = cl_apj_dt_controller=>get_instance( ).
    DATA(li_entity) = lo_controller->if_apj_dt_controller~retrieve_single(
                          is_entity_type_name = VALUE #( entity_type    = entity_type
                                                         entity_name    = name
                                                         entity_version = 0 )
                          iv_edit_mode        = if_apj_dt_types=>cs_edit_mode-exclusive ).
    ASSERT li_entity IS BOUND.
    result = NEW lcl_jt( ).
    result->name          = name.
    result->mode          = if_apj_dt_types=>cs_mode-update.
    result->data_accessor = CAST #( li_entity ).
  ENDMETHOD.

  METHOD save_and_activate.
    lcl_apj_helper=>write_tadir_entry( name              = name
                                       tadir_object_type = 'SAJT'
                                       devclass          = devclass ).
    IF trkorr IS NOT INITIAL.
      lcl_apj_helper=>set_default_transport( trkorr ).
    ENDIF.

    IF mode = if_apj_dt_types=>cs_mode-create.
      DATA(object_data) = NEW cl_apj_dt_gui_tmp( ).
      object_data->if_wb_object_data_model~generate_from_key( EXACT #( name ) ).

      DATA(persist) = NEW cl_apj_wb_jt_persist( ).
      persist->if_wb_object_persist~save( p_object_data = object_data
                                          p_access_mode = if_wb_adt_rest_resource_co=>co_access_mode_insert ).
    ENDIF.

    DATA(lo_controller) = cl_apj_dt_controller=>get_instance( ).
    lo_controller->modify_single( is_modify = VALUE #( mode       = if_apj_dt_types=>cs_mode-update
                                                       entity_ref = data_accessor ) ).

    " Save and activate
    " TODO: variable is assigned but never used (ABAP cleaner)
    DATA(is_success) = lo_controller->save( ).
    lo_controller->activate( it_entity_type_and_name = VALUE #( ( entity_type = entity_type
                                                                  entity_name = name ) ) ).
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

entry.title: Re: SSO Error in SAC
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I wanted to provide a quick follow-up regarding the configuration.

1. Configuration:

  • Azure:

    • NameID = user.principalname

  • IAS:

    • Subject Name Identifier (SNI) = Corporate Identity Provider = NameID

  • SAC:

    • USER ID = employeeid

In this configuration, the user is able to authenticate without any issues.

2. Issue with New Configuration:

However, when I change the configuration to the following:

  • Azure:

    • NameID = user.principalname

  • IAS:

    • SNI = Corporate Identity Provider = employeeid

I receive the following error message:

It seems that you don't have an active account. Please contact your system administrator and ensure that you have an active account for this system.

USER ID in SAC

 

How to resolve this issue?

Thank you in advance for your help !!!

Best regards

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Schedule and monitor application related jobs
entry.content:
One more information, about how to schedule Application Jobs from a custom program.

The recommended and documented way is to use this class:

CL_APJ_RT_API

In old systems, this class may not exist, instead this class may be used:

CL_APJ_RT_JOB_SCHEDULING_API

entry.title: Re: Best practice recommendations on "Save all objects to...
entry.content:
Hi Aravind,

This KBA provides a solution to your issue with synchronization timeout.  My only advice is to set the value of each of the Registry Entries to approximately 480000 instead of 120000, as listed in the KBA.  You will need to restart your PC after implementing the Registry Entries to see the change.

Outside that, my personal preference is to only download the content structure when synchronizing large Workspaces.  Turn on the Check out folder structure only field when using the Workspace > Check Out Objects functionality.

If you have the folder structure in Producer, just right click a folder that contains content that you want to work on and select Server Bulk Actions > Update All Objects from Server.  This will allow you to download the content for that folder, rather than the entire Workspace.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud - Manufacturing Certification
entry.content:
Hello @shahmurari_1986 ,

Thanks for your question. SAP offers certification courses for various modules and editions of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, including Manufacturing in the Public Cloud.

To find the specific certification course for Manufacturing in the Public Cloud.  you can visit the official SAP Training and Certification Shop. Here are the general steps you can follow:

1. Visit the SAP Training and Certification Shop: Go to the official SAP training website at https://training.sap.com/.

2. Search for Courses: Use the search bar to look for courses related to "SAP S/4HANA Cloud Manufacturing" or "Manufacturing in Public Cloud ".

3. Filter by Edition: Make sure to filter the search results to find courses specific to the 2408 edition. You can usually find this information in the course description or title.

4. Review Course Details: Look at the course details, including the curriculum, duration, and certification exam information to ensure it matches your needs.

5. Enroll in the Course: Once you find the appropriate course, you can enroll directly through the SAP Training and Certification Shop.

If you have trouble finding the specific course, you can also reach out to SAP's support or training team for more personalized assistance. They can provide you with the most up-to-date information on available courses and certifications for the 2408 edition.

Hope it can help.

Best Regards

Dave
